mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds +0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, indicating rising rates predict positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or decoupling of RSAFS and HOUST growth yields positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, leading to negative equity returns as earnings forecasts are downgraded over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines predict positive returns during tightening periods, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a regime-filtered backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, the cost of capital becomes prohibitive for highly leveraged firms, causing a broad market sell-off and negative forward returns over the next week as liquidity tightens.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS crosses 4.5%, or the 5-day forward return distribution shows no significant negative skew.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate indicates a healthy labor market expansion that boosts consumer confidence and corporate hiring, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative IC over a 3-year window, or rising PAYEMS with stable UNRATE predicts negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate profit margins, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating GDP growth predicts negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation coupled with a rising federal funds rate creates a stagflationary pressure that compresses real discount rates and erodes purchasing power, resulting in negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses predictive power in a multivariate regression.
